contentBlocks.original:
रामाय रामभद्राय रामचंद्राय वेधसे ।
रघुनाथाय नाथाय सीतायाः पतये नमः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmāya rāmabhadrāya rāmacaṃdrāya vedhase .
raghunāthāya nāthāya sītāyāḥ pataye namaḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to Rama who is also known as Ramabhadra and Ramachandra, who is mentioned in Vedas as the greatest lord of Raghuvansha and the whole world, and who is the consort of Sita.
(or)
I salute that Rama who is benevolent (good and protecting) and peaceful as the moon, and who is the lord of Sita and the master guardian of all.
contentBlocks.meanings: रामाय (rāmāya) – to Rama; रामभद्राय (rāmabhadrāya) – to Ramabhadra (rāma:Rama | bhadra:auspicious); रामचंद्राय (rāmacaṃdrāya) – to Ramachandra (rāma:Rama | caṃdra:moon); वेधसे (vedhase) – to the creator; रघुनाथाय (raghunāthāya) – to the lord of Raghu (raghu:Raghu | nātha:lord); नाथाय (nāthāya) – to the lord; सीतायाः (sītāyāḥ) – of Sita; पतये (pataye) – to the husband; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;
